title: cracca multifolia
intro: Cracca multifolia, also known as the Stickseed or Lupine, is a perennial plant that belongs to the legume family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its saponin and alkaloid content. The health benefits of Cracca multifolia include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, decoction, or infusion for its medicinal uses, and its dried roots and leaves can also be used to make herbal supplements.
